narrative: Jindal is the overall leader on absolute water withdrawal and consumption, while JSW is the operational leader on physical intensity at 2.39 kL/tcs. SAIL ranks 2nd on absolute withdrawal but 3rd on consumption and physical intensity, with 3.02 kL/tcs. Tata ranks last on total withdrawal and consumption, and 3rd on physical intensity at 4.40 kL/tcs. On discharge, JSW and Jindal report zero total discharge, while Tata reports 14.01 million kL and SAIL 22.99 million kL. Tata’s FY2023-24 BRSR context shows a water-scarcity mitigation approach based on Reduce, Reuse, Recycle and Recover, including ZED aspirations, municipal wastewater reuse, water harvesting, CETP/STP augmentation and reuse in coke quenching, BF slag granulation, gas cleaning and dust suppression. That strategy is directionally strong, but FY2023-24 data still shows a gap versus zero-discharge peers.
